dataset.description: Searches for a specific target value in an array and returns its index, or -1 if not found.
dataset.pseudocode:
BEGIN
  DECLARE data AS ARRAY
  data = [15, 22, 8, 42, 17]
  DECLARE target AS INTEGER
  target = 8
  DECLARE foundIndex AS INTEGER
  foundIndex = -1
  DECLARE i AS INTEGER
  FOR i FROM 0 TO 4 DO
    IF data[i] == target THEN
      foundIndex = i
    ENDIF
  ENDFOR
  PRINT foundIndex
END
dataset.python_code:
data = [15, 22, 8, 42, 17]
target = 8
foundIndex = -1
for i in range(0, 4 + 1):
    if data[i] == target:
        foundIndex = i
print(foundIndex)

dataset.description: Continuously reads user integer input into an array until a 0 is entered, then calculates the sum.
dataset.pseudocode:
BEGIN
  DECLARE collection AS ARRAY
  collection = []
  DECLARE sum AS INTEGER
  sum = 0
  DECLARE val AS INTEGER
  INPUT val
  WHILE val <> 0 DO
    APPEND val TO collection
    sum = sum + val
    INPUT val
  ENDWHILE
  PRINT sum
END
dataset.python_code:
collection = []
sum = 0
val = int(input())
while val != 0:
    collection.append(val)
    sum = sum + val
    val = int(input())
print(sum)

dataset.description: Creates a new array containing only negative numbers from the original array.
dataset.pseudocode:
BEGIN
  DECLARE source AS ARRAY
  source = [5, -2, 9, -1, 0, -4]
  DECLARE negatives AS ARRAY
  negatives = []
  DECLARE i AS INTEGER
  FOR i FROM 0 TO 5 DO
    IF source[i] < 0 THEN
      APPEND source[i] TO negatives
    ENDIF
  ENDFOR
  PRINT negatives
END

dataset.description: Prints a grid of coordinates, replacing coordinates where x equals y with a string indicator.
dataset.pseudocode:
BEGIN
  DECLARE x AS INTEGER
  DECLARE y AS INTEGER
  FOR y FROM 1 TO 3 DO
    FOR x FROM 1 TO 3 DO
      IF x == y THEN
        PRINT "Match"
      ELSE
        PRINT x + y
      ENDIF
    ENDFOR
  ENDFOR
END

dataset.description: Iterates through two arrays simultaneously to calculate total cost (quantity * price).
dataset.pseudocode:
BEGIN
  DECLARE quantities AS ARRAY
  quantities = [2, 5, 1]
  DECLARE prices AS ARRAY
  prices = [10, 4, 50]
  DECLARE totalCost AS INTEGER
  totalCost = 0
  DECLARE i AS INTEGER
  FOR i FROM 0 TO 2 DO
    totalCost = totalCost + (quantities[i] * prices[i])
  ENDFOR
  PRINT totalCost
END
dataset.python_code:
quantities = [2, 5, 1]
prices = [10, 4, 50]
totalCost = 0
for i in range(0, 2 + 1):
    totalCost = totalCost + (quantities[i] * prices[i])
print(totalCost)

dataset.concept: Finding Minimum Value via While Loop
dataset.description: Finds the smallest positive integer out of exactly 5 inputs using a while loop and strict scoping.
dataset.pseudocode:
BEGIN
  DECLARE minValue AS INTEGER
  minValue = 99999
  DECLARE currentVal AS INTEGER
  DECLARE count AS INTEGER
  count = 0
  WHILE count < 5 DO
    INPUT currentVal
    IF currentVal < minValue THEN
      minValue = currentVal
    ENDIF
    count = count + 1
  ENDWHILE
  PRINT minValue
END
dataset.python_code:
minValue = 99999
count = 0
while count < 5:
    currentVal = int(input())
    if currentVal < minValue:
        minValue = currentVal
    count = count + 1
print(minValue)

dataset.description: Iterates over an array and doubles the value of every element in place.
dataset.pseudocode:
BEGIN
  DECLARE arr AS ARRAY
  arr = [1, 2, 3, 4]
  DECLARE i AS INTEGER
  FOR i FROM 0 TO 3 DO
    arr[i] = arr[i] * 2
  ENDFOR
  PRINT arr
END
dataset.python_code:
arr = [1, 2, 3, 4]
for i in range(0, 3 + 1):
    arr[i] = arr[i] * 2
print(arr)

dataset.description: Combines strings from an array into a single greeting message.
dataset.pseudocode:
BEGIN
  DECLARE words AS ARRAY
  words = ["Hello", "World", "Python", "Rules"]
  DECLARE sentence AS STRING
  sentence = ""
  DECLARE i AS INTEGER
  FOR i FROM 0 TO 3 DO
    sentence = sentence + words[i]
    IF i < 3 THEN
      sentence = sentence + " "
    ENDIF
  ENDFOR
  PRINT sentence
END
dataset.python_code:
words = ["Hello", "World", "Python", "Rules"]
sentence = ""
for i in range(0, 3 + 1):
    sentence = sentence + words[i]
    if i < 3:
        sentence = sentence + " "
print(sentence)

dataset.description: Checks an array for instances where the same number appears twice in a row.
dataset.pseudocode:
BEGIN
  DECLARE nums AS ARRAY
  nums = [5, 8, 8, 2, 9, 9, 4]
  DECLARE hasDuplicates AS BOOLEAN
  hasDuplicates = FALSE
  DECLARE i AS INTEGER
  FOR i FROM 0 TO 5 DO
    IF nums[i] == nums[i + 1] THEN
      hasDuplicates = TRUE
    ENDIF
  ENDFOR
  PRINT hasDuplicates
END

dataset.description: Enforces type casting on input specifically to verify string concatenation versus integer addition logic inside a loop.
dataset.pseudocode:
BEGIN
  DECLARE userChoice AS INTEGER
  INPUT userChoice
  IF userChoice == 1 THEN
    PRINT "Numeric Logic"
  ELSE
    PRINT "Fallback"
  ENDIF
  DECLARE i AS INTEGER
  FOR i FROM 1 TO 3 DO
    userChoice = userChoice + 10
    PRINT userChoice
  ENDFOR
END
